# @byted-service/metrics

[![bnpm](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/badge/v/@byted-service/metrics.svg?style=flat-square)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/metrics) [![download](https://2y8ngapf.fn.bytedance.net/d?p=@byted-service/metrics)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/metrics)

[简体中文](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/metrics/README.md) | [English](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/metrics/README.en.md) | [CHANGELOG](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/metrics/CHANGELOG.md)

[Metrics instructions](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/space/doc/GHFmzle2R6a7cGvAqMWlbc)

Excerpts from several important points in the document:

-   The tags keys `env_type`, `pod_name`, `_psm`, `deploy_stage`, `host_v6`, and `cluster` are the default keys of the SDK. Do not use them in custom tags, otherwise the data will be Will be overwritten!
-   When printing, you must first specify a metric name, the length limit is 1-255, the allowed characters are `a-zA-Z0-9 ._-`. Recommended format: at least three-stage
-   You can add multiple tags to the dot to be marked. Each tag consists of a tagkey and a tagvalue.
-   The length limit of tagkey and tagvalue is 1-255(tagvalue cannot be empty, if it is empty, the dot instruction will be ignored by the agent), the allowed characters are `a-zA-Z0-9 ._- /`, for Chinese Non-asci characters can be typed using base16 encoding.
-   Note:
        1. The combined value (Cartesian product) of all tags of a certain metric should not exceed 100w. (Special needs require special communication)
        2. Remember not to enter uid-like tag values into the metrics system

## Installation

```bash
npm install @byted-service/metrics --registry=http://bnpm.byted.org
```

## Create Metrics instance

```js
const Metrics = require('@byted-service/metrics');
const metrics = new Metrics(options);
```

options

-   `debug`: Whether to enable debug mode. In this mode, a dot log will be output to the console. The default is false.
-   `silentVerifying`: When there are illegal characters in the tag, the prompt of`console.warn` will not be performed, the default is false
-   `host`: Metric Server host address, default is 127.0.0.1
-   `port`: Metric Server host port, default is 9123
-   `defaultPrefix`: default prefix for metric point names, default is toutiao
-   `bufferSize`: The buffer size. The default value is 1, the maximum value is 500. If it exceeds 500, the maximum value is used.
-   `flushInterval`: Flush buffer interval, unit is ms.
-   `enablePreAggregate`: Enable pre-aggregation. The default value is false, which means it is not enabled. The metrics data is aggregated on the SDK side. To enable this option, the tag key must be predefined. For more details, please see [documents](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/docs/doccnW90g3m9WLGcdUrBndnRtOg#), at the same time, in order to prevent the data from jittering, please set the parameter `flushInterval` to an approximate number of 30(s)
-   `unixDgram`: The default is false. If your application is in unix system you can set unixDgram true to use unix-datagram send metrics data. In the same time you can also set bufferSize bigger like 5 that is good for perfomance.`Node.js version must be >= 10.0.0`
-   `disableBuiltinClusterTag`: Whether to remove the built-in cluster tag, the default is false.

## View metrics data when local develop

Report metrics need agent, but you haven't agent when develop on local. And metrics connect to agent by UDP Socket, so the send action will failed silently on local.

You can set environment variable `ROUTE_IP` or set options `host` to forward metrics report, if you want to view the data when develop on local. [This document](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/consul/README.md#%E5%A6%82%E4%BD%95%E8%8E%B7%E5%8F%96-boe-%E7%8E%AF%E5%A2%83%E7%9A%84-ip-%E5%9C%B0%E5%9D%80) will help you to get valid agent IP.

If you got any alarm on BOE because metrics report on local, you can set environment variable `METRICS_ENABLE=false` to make metrics library NOT send report to server(agent).

Actually, the `METRICS_ENABLE` will only effect SEND, you can also get log on local-dev by set options `debug: true`.

## Rush

See https://bytedance.feishu.cn/docs/GHFmzle2R6a7cGvAqMWlbc for the description of these different types.

```js
metrics.emitCounter(name, value, prefix, tags);

metrics.emitTimer(name, value, prefix, tags);

metrics.emitStore(name, value, prefix, tags);

// Added after 1.10.0
metrics.emitRateCounter(name, value, prefix, tags);

// Added after 1.10.0
metrics.emitMeter(name, value, prefix, tags);
```

Parameter Description

-   `name`: Dot name
-   `value`: tick value
-   `prefix`: Dot name prefix, if not specified, use defaultPrefix
-   `tags`: dot tag key-value pairs

## examples

```js
const Metrics = require('@byted-service/metrics');

const metrics = new Metrics({
    defaultPrefix: 'ies.fe',
    bufferSize: 10,
    flushInterval: 3 * 1000,
});

metrics.emitCounter('byted-service.metrics.test.from.local', 1, 'ies.fe', {
    name: 'test',
});
```

## Data query authorization

**Note:** Functions to query metrics data through open api in this lib only fit old version of metrics open api. And these functions will be removed from this lib in the future. Please refer to [metrics-oapi](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/metrics-oapi) and [official documents](https://doc.bytedance.net/docs/2080/2717/29482/) if you need the feature.

_since: 1.4.0_

After applying, you can get the token through the Auth instance:

```js
const { Auth } = require('@byted-service/metrics');

const auth = new Auth({
    region: 'cn',
    app_name: 'YOUR_APP_NAME',
    app_secret: 'YOUR_APP_SECERT',
});

const token = await auth.getToken();
```

### Auth.getToken(): Promise \<string\>

Get the token. If there is no token or the token expires, it will bring data to request the token; otherwise, it will directly return the local token.

### Auth.updateToken(): Promise \<undefined\>

Update the token. If the token does not expire, the token returned by the metrics server may not change.

## data query

```js
const { Auth, query } = require('@byted-service/metrics');

const auth = new Auth({
    region: 'cn',
    app_name: 'YOUR_APP_NAME',
    app_secret: 'YOUR_APP_SECERT',
});

const options = {
    // NOTE Please make sure that the region is the same as the region in Auth, otherwise the request will fail
    region: 'cn',
    timeout: 0, // If no auth instance is passed in, the query will not take a token, and the query may fail
    auth,
};

(async () => {
    try {
        const data = await query(
            {
                start: Date.now() - 5 * 60 * 1000, // Aggregate multiple queries in one request
                queries: [
                    {
                        metric: 'toutiao.byted-service.metrics.test.from.local',
                        aggregator: 'avg',
                    },
                ],
            },
            options
        );
    } catch (err) {
        // Request failed
    }
})();
```

Parameter Description:

-   `query`: query parameters, please refer to [definition](./src/query.ts) for data structure
-   `options`: request parameter setting, default is:

```js
{
    // idc_region
    region: 'cn',
    // Request timeout timeout
    timeout: 0,
    // query interface
    prefix: 'http://metrics.byted.org/proxy/api/query',
}
```

### Use `<amount> <time unit> -ago` time format in queries

Opentsdb can specify the time format `<amount> <time unit> -ago` to conveniently replace the timestamp, such as:`4h-ago`. Because the current company's post-style query interface does not support using this time format to replace the `start` and`end` parameters in the form of timestamp, so this lib provides `parseTimeString()` for conversion:

```js
const { parseTimeString } = require('@byted-service/metrics');

parseTimeString('4h-ago'); // 1548819456410
```

Use in `query`:

```js
const { query, parseTimeString } = require('@byted-service/metrics');

query({
    start: parseTimeString('4h-ago'),
    end: parseTimeString('2h-ago'),
    queries: [],
});
```

Related documents can be found at: [Dates and Times](http://opentsdb.net/docs/build/html/user_guide/query/dates.html)
